severity_rationale: api.23andme.com's enforced CSP is effectively nil (default-src *, script-src * unsafe-inline unsafe-eval). Also a report-only strict policy exists (toothless). Standalone: P5 (CSP absence is defense-in-depth, excluded as standalone under low_impact_missing_headers). REPORTABLE angle: the endpoints that return HTML on api.23andme.com (error pages, /authorize/ UI surface) render without CSP — any future HTML-XSS here goes unblocked. Elevated to P4 because this is a chain-enabler to F-F004 and any future api-host reflected-XSS.
validation_method: Curl — header inspection across multiple api.23andme.com endpoints.
response_summary: Enforced CSP: default-src * data:; script-src * 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'; style-src * 'unsafe-inline'. Report-Only header is strict but toothless. report-uri points to 3rd-party host.
impact: Defense-in-depth gap. Any HTML-returning api.23andme.com surface (error 500/404 HTML, /authorize/ UI) that reflects user input becomes unblocked XSS.
chain: Chain enabler for any future api.23andme.com HTML-XSS finding.
remediation: Apply the existing Report-Only policy as enforcing policy on api.23andme.com HTML responses. Move report-uri to a first-party endpoint.

reportability_audit.Q2_not_excluded: BORDERLINE — api CSP-absence would normally be excluded; retained because api.23andme.com serves HTML in some paths and report-only is deployed but not enforcing (indicates intent — partial rollout)
reportability_audit.Q4_not_low_class_solo: DEMOTED — this would normally be q4_low_class_solo (permissive_csp); RETAINED as P4 only because the chain-enabler angle to F-F004 overrides solo demotion. If HackerOne triage rejects as solo, resubmit only as part of an XSS chain.
